headline: Paris Mayor Grégoire unveils plan to tackle sexual violence in after-school programs: 'I want to guarantee complete transparency for families'
description: Paris's new mayor outlines his measures, including transparency, enhanced training and stricter oversight. Starting in April, a citizens' convention will debate the organization of the school week and day, including the current 4.5-day week for children.

headline: Mysterious 'knocker' continues haunting elderly women in Brittany town

10 years of the Panama Papers: The investigation that brought in €275 million for France
description: The vast majority of funds recovered by French tax authorities after the massive 2016 data leak, revealed by Le Monde and more than other 100 media outlets working with the International Consortium of Investigative Journalists, came from investigations into 230 taxpayers, both individuals and legal entities.

headline: French and South Korean presidents say will work together on Strait of Hormuz
description: On a visit to South Korea, French President Emmanuel Macron told his counterpart Lee Jae Myung that the two countries can play a role in helping to stabilize the situation in the Middle East, including the Strait of Hormuz, according to South Korean media.

headline: TotalEnergies traders reap unprecedented profits off Middle East war
description: The French major's oil-trading subsidiary made a particularly lucrative move by speculating on a closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: 'I know what happens to thieves here': Public lynchings on the rise in Kenya
description: Kenya's Interior Minister Kipchumba Murkomen revealed on February 25 that 579 people were killed in 2025 due to urban violence. A few months earlier, he acknowledged the police's responsibility in these incidents of 'mob justice.'

'Refusing to divide French society into the racialized and the non-racialized does not mean denying the existence of racism'
description: Sociologist Stéphane Beaud and historian Gérard Noiriel analyze why people who have experienced racism – such as Bally Bagayoko, Saint-Denis's new mayor – may reject the label 'racialized.'

headline: The wild story of a new Molière play, co-written with AI
description: For nearly three years, the Molière Sorbonne Theater in Paris and a trio of artists have been working to produce a comedy, 'The Astrologer or the False Omens,' that the playwright might have written had he lived longer.

headline: Sarkozy appeal trial hears moving testimonies from families of UTA flight bombing victims
description: On Wednesday, 13 relatives of victims killed in the 1989 plane bombing took to the witness stand to express their anger at former French president Nicolas Sarkozy. He is accused of making a pact with the Libyan masterminds of the bombing to receive campaign funding.
